reasoning: The value of the Overnight Bank Funding Rate on 2025-06-22 is exactly 4.33, where it has remained completely flat since early January 2025 following a series of rate cuts in late 2024. For the rate to have 'increased' by the resolution dates, the FOMC would either need to hike interest rates (raising the target range above 4.25%-4.50%) or the OBFR would need to technically drift upwards by at least 1 basis point (to 4.34 or higher). According to CME FedWatch Tool data from June 2025, markets widely expect the Fed to either hold rates steady or enact further rate cuts through the end of 2025, with between 0 and 4 expected cuts remaining. A rate hike is currently viewed as highly improbable, though tail-risk probabilities for a hike increase toward the end of the year if inflation unexpectedly rebounds. Historically, OBFR strictly follows the Fed Funds target range, though single-basis-point drifts do rarely happen (e.g. 5.32 to 5.33 in Sept 2024). Because the most likely outcomes (hold or cut) both result in the rate not increasing (resolving NO), the probability of an increase is extremely low for the near-term dates and only rises slightly for the late-year dates to account for macroeconomic uncertainty and potential rate drift.
